title: Tesla (TSLA) Q2 2026 delivery consensus: 406,000 vehicles expected
summary:
Tesla released its company-compiled delivery consensus for the second quarter of 2026, and Wall Street analysts now expect the automaker to deliver 406,024 vehicles.

That would be just 5.7% growth over the 384,122 vehicles Tesla delivered in Q2 2025 — a modest recovery for a company that has posted two consecutive years of declining sales.

title: The Jeep Recon EV goes on sale for $67,000 with a limited range
summary: Jeep’s electric off-roader is now on sale, starting at $67,000, but its driving range comes up a bit short of expectations.

title: BYD’s pickup spotted in China with a different look
summary: BYD’s plug-in hybrid pickup is already a hit in several overseas markets, but a new prototype was spotted driving in China with a slightly different design.

title: Podcast: Slate’s $25,000 electric pickup, Polestar banned from US, Tesla FSD crash, and more
summary: In the Electrek Podcast, we discuss the most popular news in the world of sustainable transport and energy. In this week’s episode, we discuss Slate’s $25,000 electric pickup, Polestar being banned from the US, Tesla FSD crashes coming into focus, and more.

title: Slate’s electric truck can still dip under $20,000 — but only for some
summary:
Slate Auto’s electric truck now starts at $24,950, but some buyers will still drive one home for under $20,000 thanks to state-level EV rebates.

The catch is that those discounts depend heavily on where you live and how much you earn — and several of them may not apply to Slate at all.

title: Final Prime Day Deals: Segway GT3 Pro superscooter first discount, EcoFlow 3,600Wh power station at $1,399 2026 low, more
summary:
It’s the final day of Amazon’s ongoing Prime Day savings, and we have no clue how much longer a lot of the deals will last after today, so be sure to get your picks in before they’re gone. Headlining this final edition is Segway’s first-ever discount on the monstrous GT3 Pro Electric SuperScooter to $2,500. Right behind it, we have EcoFlow’s updated Prime Day flash sale lineup, which has dropped the 3,600Wh DELTA Pro Portable Power Station to its $1,399 2026 low, as well as Tenways’ July 4th Super Sale that has restocked the CGO600 Pro Commuter e-bike with a $118 FREE bundle at $1,599. We also have a selection of favorites from EcoFlow, EGO Power+, Greenworks, Beatbot, Anker SOLIX, and more all waiting for you below – plus, you can find all the best deals from brands we couldn’t stuff in here within our one-stop Prime Day Green Deals hub here.

Head below for other New Green Deals we’ve found today and, of course, Electrek’s best EV buying and leasing deals. Also, check out the new Electrek Tesla Shop for the best deals on Tesla accessories.

title: GM’s new EV platform is expected to debut in the next-gen Chevy Equinox
summary: The new version of GM’s dedicated electric vehicle platform is reportedly set to arrive near the end of 2028, and the Chevy Equinox EV will be the first to ride on it.

title: This Canadian team has raced solar cars since 1992, and they’re bringing their best yet to the 2026 Electrek American Solar Challenge
summary:
This July, the 2026 Electrek Formula Sun Grand Prix (FSGP) and American Solar Challenge (ASC) head north to Minnesota. Meanwhile, I caught up with one of the teams making the trip, and they’ve been racing solar cars longer than almost anyone. Éclipse, out of École de technologie supérieure (ÉTS) in Montreal, has been building them for over 33 years, since 1992. Hence the big 92 you’ll spot on the car.

The team’s captain, Yohann Gourmelen, sat down with me to talk about their new car (Éclipse 12), some rule changes shaking up this year’s race, and how they build nearly an entire solar car in-house.

title: Hyundai’s small electric SUV just got a little more upscale
summary: Hyundai launched the Inster Lounge in Europe, a new range-topping model that brings a touch of luxury to the small electric SUV segment.

title: EIA: Renewables just hit 30% of US electricity generation
summary: Renewables accounted for 30.0% of total US electrical generation during the first third of 2026, up 2.2% year over year, according to new data recently released by the US Energy Information Administration (EIA), and reviewed by the SUN DAY Campaign.

title: Slate's Cheap EV Pickup Is Surprisingly Easy To Make Expensive
summary: Slate's $25,000 electric pickup is refreshingly simple, but its endless menu of accessories can quickly add up to something else.

title: Shell Built A Tiny EV With Hyper-Fast Charging And A New Cooling System
summary: This tiny EV is impressively clever, but the real business case is hiding in the coolant.

title: How Hankook Is Making EV Tires Better
summary: The company's line of iON tires is designed to maximize durability and range. You can experience them yourself at Electrify Expo Demo Days.

title: Tesla Is Giving Away Free Supercharging. Here's How To Win.
summary: Tesla is gamifying charging and will reward the winners with free Supercharging for the life of the car. Here's how to win.

title: The Peaq Is Peak Skoda. Too Bad America Can’t Buy It
summary: The Peaq is a big, sensible, seven-seat electric SUV with serious space and range and a very reasonable price.

title: Slate's $24,950 EV Truck Will Test What America Really Wants
summary: The Slate will be both the country’s cheapest new electric vehicle and its lowest-priced pickup. But will it be an automatic slam dunk?

title: Rivian Discontinues Its Cheapest R1T And R1S As R2 Rolls Out
summary: Rivian flagged earlier this year that the Dual Standard variants of its large SUV and truck were on the way out.

title: This Ex-Rental Tesla's Battery Was Heavily Degraded. Then The Decline Slowed
summary: After losing 20% of its capacity in its first two years, this abused Tesla’s battery degradation appears to have almost plateaued.

title: Rivian Has A Bold Plan For You To Skip The Repair Shop
summary: Rivian says 80% of EV repairs could eventually be handled by its mobile fleet, reducing the need for visits to physical service centers.

title: A Driverless Xiaomi Electric SUV Just Lapped The Ring. We Have Questions
summary: A 10-minute Nurburgring lap time isn't impressive. The fact that there is nobody in the driver’s seat is what makes this unique.
